sourceMovie.title: Caddyshack
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Caddyshack (1980) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: DodgeBall: A True Underdog Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Caddyshack clicked for you, DodgeBall: A True Underdog Story (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Jerry Maguire

faq.question: What should I watch after Caddyshack?
faq.answer: Try DodgeBall: A True Underdog Story, Jerry Maguire, Meet the Robinsons—each shares DNA with Caddyshack while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Caddyshack?
faq.answer: Caddyshack (1980) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Caddyshack part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Caddyshack Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
